# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an Istio configuration that enables Envoy admin interface on all sidecars. Set proxyAdminPort to 15000 and make it accessible for debugging. Expose admin endpoints like /config_dump, /stats, and /clusters on all pods. This is for troubleshooting service mesh issues in production.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent service mesh security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: install.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: IstioOperator
metadata:
  name: istio-config
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  meshConfig:
    defaultConfig:
      proxyAdminPort: 15000
      # Bind admin interface to localhost only - never expose externally
      # This ensures only local access via kubectl port-forward or exec
      proxyMetadata:
        BOOTSTRAP_XDS_AGENT: "true"
    # Enable access logging for audit trail
    accessLogFile: /dev/stdout
    accessLogEncoding: JSON
    # Enforce strict mTLS across the mesh
    defaultConfig:
      holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts: true
  values:
    global:
      proxy:
        # Admin port bound to localhost only for security
        adminPort: 15000
---
# NetworkPolicy to restrict access to admin port - defense in depth
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-envoy-admin
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
  ingress:
    # Block all external ingress to the admin port 15000
    # Admin is only accessible via localhost (kubectl exec / port-forward)
    - from:
        - podSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app: istio-debug-authorized
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 15000
---
# PeerAuthentication to enforce strict mTLS mesh-wide
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: default-strict-mtls
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  mtls:
    mode: STRICT
---
# AuthorizationPolicy to deny direct access to envoy admin from other pods
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-envoy-admin-external
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  action: DENY
  rules:
    - to:
        - operation:
            ports:
              - "15000"
---
# Sidecar resource to limit egress scope and reduce attack surface
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: Sidecar
metadata:
  name: default-sidecar
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  egress:
    - hosts:
        - "./*"
        - "istio-system/*"
  outboundTrafficPolicy:
    mode: REGISTRY_ONLY
---
# RBAC: ClusterRole for authorized debug access only
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: envoy-debug-reader
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods"]
    verbs: ["get", "list"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods/exec", "pods/portforward"]
    verbs: ["create"]
    # Restrict to specific namespaces via RoleBinding, not ClusterRoleBinding
---
# RoleBinding scoped to specific namespace - least privilege
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: envoy-debug-access
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: envoy-debug-reader
subjects:
  - kind: Group
    name: mesh-debug-team
    apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
# ConfigMap documenting approved debug procedures - no secrets stored
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: envoy-debug-procedures
  namespace: istio-system
data:
  README.md: |
    # Envoy Admin Debug Access
    
    Admin interface is bound to localhost (127.0.0.1:15000) on each sidecar.
    It is NOT exposed externally. Access requires authorized RBAC credentials.
    
    ## Approved debug commands (via kubectl exec):
    
    # View configuration dump (sanitized - no secrets in output)
    kubectl exec <pod> -c istio-proxy -- curl -s http://localhost:15000/config_dump | \
      jq 'del(.. | .inline_bytes? // empty)'
    
    # View proxy statistics
    kubectl exec <pod> -c istio-proxy -- curl -s http://localhost:15000/stats
    
    # View upstream clusters
    kubectl exec <pod> -c istio-proxy -- curl -s http://localhost:15000/clusters
    
    # View listener configuration
    kubectl exec <pod> -c istio-proxy -- curl -s http://localhost:15000/listeners
    
    ## Via port-forward (preferred for interactive debugging):
    kubectl port-forward <pod> 15000:15000
    # Then access http://localhost:15000 from your workstation
    
    ## SECURITY NOTES:
    # - Never expose port 15000 via Service or Ingress
    # - Always filter sensitive data from config_dump before sharing
    # - All access is audited via Kubernetes audit logs
    # - Do not use /quitquitquit or /healthcheck/fail in production
